title: Quiz: Quantum Chemistry Applications
description: Test your understanding of quantum chemistry on quantum computers

questions.question: Why is quantum chemistry a promising application for quantum computers?

options.text: Molecules are inherently quantum and classical simulation scales exponentially
Quantum computers are cheaper than lab experiments

questions.explanation: Molecules are quantum systems, and exact classical simulation (Full CI) scales exponentially. Quantum computers naturally simulate quantum systems.

questions.question: What is 'chemical accuracy'?
questions.explanation: Chemical accuracy is defined as energy error < 1 kcal/mol ≈ 1.6 milliHartree, which is sufficient for predicting chemical reactions.

questions.question: What does the Jordan-Wigner transformation do?

options.text: Maps fermionic operators to qubit operators
Reduces the number of qubits needed

questions.explanation: Jordan-Wigner transformation maps fermionic creation/annihilation operators to Pauli strings, enabling simulation on qubits.

questions.question: UCCSD ansatz is the most NISQ-friendly choice for quantum chemistry.
questions.explanation: False. UCCSD has O(N^4) depth making it challenging for NISQ. Hardware-efficient ansätze or QCC are more NISQ-friendly.

questions.question: How does symmetry help in quantum chemistry simulations?

options.text: It reduces qubits needed and enables error detection
It increases the number of measurements needed

questions.explanation: Symmetries (particle number, spin) can reduce the Hilbert space and enable post-selection to detect erroneous measurements.

questions.question: What molecule has been successfully simulated with chemical accuracy on quantum hardware?
questions.explanation: H2 is the simplest molecule and has been demonstrated with chemical accuracy on quantum hardware using VQE and error mitigation.

questions.question: What is ADAPT-VQE?

options.text: A fixed ansatz for all molecules
An ansatz that grows adaptively based on gradient information

questions.explanation: ADAPT-VQE builds the ansatz iteratively by adding operators with the largest gradients, creating a problem-specific circuit.

questions.question: Which are challenges for quantum chemistry on NISQ devices? (Select all)

options.text: Many Pauli terms in molecular Hamiltonians

questions.explanation: Chemistry faces challenges from deep circuits, many Hamiltonian terms requiring many measurements, and the need for high precision. Molecules have many electrons, not few.
